title: The Break-Up

jokes.text: I'm gonna mount a TV on your wall. I just can't find a stud. So you made nine holes in my wall? No, those are for the shelves, dummy.
jokes.setup: Dennis says he can't find a stud to mount the TV
jokes.punchline: He's made nine random holes but claims they're for shelves, calling Liz dummy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Dennis - incompetent but confident, creating chaos while acting superior

jokes.text: It's gonna be like a hospital.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing how awesome the wall-mounted TV will be
jokes.punchline: His aspirational comparison is to a hospital - the least desirable TV viewing experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd character moment - Dennis thinks hospital TV is aspirational, perfect economy of words

jokes.text: That's my cousin Teddy's Great Dane. I told him I'd watch him for a couple weeks. 'cause Teddy broke his ankle running from some black guys that pulled a gun on him.
jokes.setup: Seemingly normal explanation about watching a dog for injured cousin
jokes.punchline: Casually drops shocking details about the 'injury' circumstances
jokes.explanation: Sets up the brilliant reversal that follows - Dennis's casual racism revealed

jokes.text: They weren't muggers. They were cops. So why don't you just say he was running from some cops? I don't know, I mean, you're a racist for assuming that they weren't cops.
jokes.setup: Liz questions why race was relevant to the mugging story
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals they were cops and turns the racism accusation back on Liz
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection and character work - Dennis's twisted logic perfectly executed, completely unexpected reversal

jokes.text: Ack, allergies are all mental.
jokes.setup: Liz says she's allergic to dogs
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismisses allergies as mental with confident ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's confident stupidity

jokes.text: Which is it: you love me or you got squatter's rights? I don't see why they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Liz points out the contradiction in Dennis's desperate pleas
jokes.punchline: Dennis sees no conflict between love and legal technicalities
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that captures Dennis's inability to understand basic relationship concepts

jokes.text: Hi. I got donuts. What's up, brown eyes? I made chili.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Dennis's 'good moments'
jokes.punchline: His romantic gestures are just bringing food
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy showing the poverty of Dennis's romantic repertoire

jokes.text: I guess they were mostly food related.
jokes.setup: Series of Dennis bringing food as romantic gestures
jokes.punchline: Liz's dry acknowledgment of the pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery line that caps the visual sequence

jokes.text: That thing licked my eyes this morning.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her red, watery eyes
jokes.punchline: Disgusting specific detail about dog behavior
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works because of the specific, unexpected detail

jokes.text: Oh, you mean like at a discotheque?
jokes.setup: Jenna suggests going out to meet people
jokes.punchline: Liz's outdated reference reveals her social isolation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's social awkwardness expressed through outdated terminology

jokes.text: Allergies are all in the mind, Lemon. I use to have a wicked peanut allergy. And now, witness.
jokes.setup: Jack confidently states allergies are mental and claims he overcame his peanut allergy
jokes.punchline: Presumably eats peanuts to demonstrate (visual gag)
jokes.explanation: Callback to Dennis's line but elevated by Jack's corporate confidence

jokes.text: Are you dating Condoleeza Rice? I'm not at liberty to say.
jokes.setup: Jack hints at dating a high-ranking African-American Bush administration member
jokes.punchline: Liz correctly guesses Condoleeza Rice; Jack's non-denial confirms it
jokes.explanation: Absurdist premise perfectly executed - Jack would absolutely think he could date Condoleeza Rice

jokes.text: Yeah, and it must be hard for her to get cell phone reception when she's so far up the president's butt.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions his girlfriend travels a lot for work
jokes.punchline: Liz's crude political insult about Condoleeza Rice's loyalty to Bush
jokes.explanation: Solid political humor that fits Liz's character and the show's liberal perspective

jokes.text: I'm choosing to ignore that remark on the basis that you are a godless, glassy-eyed Clintonista.
jokes.setup: Liz makes a crude political joke
jokes.punchline: Jack's overly formal dismissal with creative political insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack dialogue - formal, condescending, and creatively insulting

jokes.text: this collection of ladies' unmentionables with snaps and openings all over the place
jokes.setup: Jack asking for gift advice for his sophisticated girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His formal description of obviously inappropriate lingerie
jokes.explanation: Jack's formal language makes the inappropriate gift suggestion funnier

jokes.text: Rodney, don't make me come over there and beat you in the head with one of my boom-booms!
jokes.setup: Tracy performing as female character Shamanda
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous dialogue with euphemistic threat using 'boom-booms' for breasts
jokes.explanation: Silly drag comedy that sets up the serious discussion about racial representation

jokes.text: Hunky Grandma Be Trippin' made $96 million.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending his history of playing women
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous movie title that's clearly a parody of Tyler Perry-style films
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody title that captures the absurdity of certain comedy franchises

jokes.text: Black Frasier? Niles, this Beaujolais is impeccable. It's both fruity and precocious. That's not the only thing around here that's fruity and precocious.
jokes.setup: Toofer mentions working on 'Black Frasier'
jokes.punchline: Perfect parody of Frasier dialogue with the expected innuendo punchline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody that perfectly captures Frasier's pretentious dialogue and gay panic humor

jokes.text: Every Thursday night at about 9:00 or 9:15.
jokes.setup: Describing when Black Frasier aired on BET
jokes.punchline: The vague time suggests BET's inconsistent scheduling
jokes.explanation: Smart joke about network scheduling differences that feels authentic

jokes.text: Bernie Mac doesn't do it 'cause he'd be ugly as hell.
jokes.setup: Listing respected Black comedians who don't do drag
jokes.punchline: Bernie Mac's reason is purely aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective punchline that undercuts the serious point with shallow humor

jokes.text: I'll do it. I mean, my parents raised me as a girl for, like, ten years. What? I told you guys that.
jokes.setup: Need someone to do the female character in sketch
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual revelation about her bizarre childhood, acting like she's mentioned it before
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - shocking revelation delivered as if it's common knowledge

jokes.text: I know this great karaoke place where you can get a pedicure while you sing.
jokes.setup: Jenna planning girls' night activities
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous combination of services that perfectly fits Jenna's character
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd venue that captures Jenna's self-centered approach to friendship

jokes.text: When I'm right here, you need to be here. Not here. Here is embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Jenna giving Liz karaoke performance advice
jokes.punchline: Visual demonstration of embarrassing vs. acceptable performance levels
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy that works through Jenna's completely unhelpful performance coaching

jokes.text: Whoopi Goldberg does it every day!
jokes.setup: Argument about Black comedians wearing dresses
jokes.punchline: Whoopi Goldberg literally wears dresses because she's a woman
jokes.explanation: Clever literal interpretation that undercuts the serious debate with absurd logic

jokes.text: I wanna hold a mirror up to society, and then win world record for biggest mirror.
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about meaningful comedy that makes people think
jokes.punchline: His goal shifts from artistic to completely literal and self-aggrandizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - starts with pretentious artistic statement, ends with absurd literal interpretation

jokes.text: You and Jenna play Sleestaks from Land of the Lost and you're trying to get a small business loan. Because you want to open up a pancake house called Slee's Stacks.
jokes.setup: Liz pitching a sketch concept
jokes.punchline: Elaborate pun-based premise involving obscure 70s TV characters
jokes.explanation: Classic bad comedy writer idea - too complicated, relies on pun and obscure reference

jokes.text: Are you black?
jokes.setup: Liz's terrible sketch pitch involving racial themes
jokes.punchline: Tracy's blunt question highlighting the obvious problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - three words that completely deflate Liz's premise

jokes.text: Next time I see Putin, I'm gonna kick his teeth in.
jokes.setup: Jack suspects his girlfriend is cheating
jokes.punchline: He threatens Vladimir Putin, revealing the absurd scale of his romantic rivalry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - reveals Jack thinks he's competing with world leaders for Condoleeza Rice

jokes.text: Well, now you're just being patronizzle.
jokes.setup: Toofer reading about -izzle suffix in urban dictionary
jokes.punchline: Applies it to 'patronizing' in completely wrong context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Toofer's academic approach to street slang

jokes.text: Standing there with your pants that fit. Using a wallet. Drinking Starbucks.
jokes.setup: Tracy criticizing Toofer for not being authentically Black
jokes.punchline: Lists completely normal behaviors as evidence of selling out
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of authenticity policing through absurd examples of 'selling out'

jokes.text: Yeah, I think they're from the firm of Date-Rape, Coke-ington Cheeseball, and Jag.
jokes.setup: Jenna asks if guys at bar are Wall Street types
jokes.punchline: Fake law firm name incorporating negative stereotypes about finance bros
jokes.explanation: Clever construction of fake law firm that captures all the negative stereotypes efficiently

jokes.text: Oh, really, dude? I gotta move my coat? There's like four empty seats down there. Can't you just be cool?
jokes.setup: Guy trying to sit next to Liz at bar
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves Jenna's characterization right with entitled behavior
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that immediately validates Jenna's prediction about these guys' behavior.

jokes.text: Do you think he'd buy me mozzarella sticks?
jokes.setup: Guy wants to buy Liz a drink
jokes.punchline: Liz's food-focused counter-request reveals her priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment and callback - Liz learned romance from Dennis

jokes.text: You could put a long, blonde wig on a ferret and it would like Jessica Simpson.
jokes.setup: Guy comparing someone to Jessica Simpson
jokes.punchline: Absurd ferret comparison showing low standards
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist imagery. The ferret comparison is specific and visual enough to work.

jokes.text: You could put a long, blonde wig on a ferret and it would like Jessica Simpson.
jokes.setup: Guy saying someone looks like Jessica Simpson
jokes.punchline: Liz's harsh and specific animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Unnecessarily cruel but funny insult that shows Liz's mean streak when drinking

jokes.text: I learned the truth at seventeen. That love was meant for beauty queens. And high school girls with clear skin smiles. Who married young and then retired.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to connect with a guy at the bar
jokes.punchline: She drunkenly sings depressing Janis Ian song
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Liz's social dysfunction expressed through inappropriate song choice

jokes.text: Mr. Jordan called me... the N-word. And? And I was really offended. Because... Because it's a racial slur.
jokes.setup: HR investigating Tracy's harassment of Toofer
jokes.punchline: HR person's casual reaction to racial slur complaint
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy about workplace racism handled with appropriate awkwardness

jokes.text: You might as well spread me down with a fire hose.
jokes.setup: Toofer upset about Tracy using the N-word
jokes.punchline: Overdramatic comparison to civil rights era violence
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate overreaction that shows Toofer's academic approach to racial issues

jokes.text: Dear Liz Lemon, While other women have bigger boobs than you, no woman has as big a heart.
jokes.setup: Dennis reading romantic letter
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing heart size to breast size
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis attempt at romance that immediately insults. Classic character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I cried like a big dumb homo.
jokes.setup: Dennis reading heartfelt letter about crying over their breakup
jokes.punchline: Uses homophobic slur to describe his own emotional response
jokes.explanation: Character-accurate but problematic humor showing Dennis's casual bigotry

jokes.text: But there's a new thing called 'women's liberation' which gives you women the right to choose, and you have chosen to abort me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining the breakup
jokes.punchline: Uses abortion metaphor for being dumped
jokes.explanation: Appropriately inappropriate Dennis logic mixing women's rights with breakup metaphors. Good character writing.

jokes.text: you have chosen to abort me
jokes.setup: Dennis's letter about women's liberation giving Liz choices
jokes.punchline: Compares breakup to abortion in tone-deaf metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate metaphor that captures Dennis's cluelessness about sensitivity

jokes.text: Hey. Threesome? How about it, huh? Mmm!
jokes.setup: Flashback to Dennis's 'bad moments'
jokes.punchline: His romantic low point is crudely propositioning threesome
jokes.explanation: Appropriately gross character moment that justifies Liz's decision to break up

jokes.text: It's how I decided to get a scooter instead of a bike.
jokes.setup: Jenna suggesting pro/con list for major life decisions
jokes.punchline: Trivial example of using this decision-making method
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jenna applies serious methodology to trivial decisions.

jokes.text: It's like my cousin Teddy's dog. Sometimes he just doesn't wanna lick my feet. So what I do is, I hide my feet from him for a couple of days, and then when he sees them, he goes bananas.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving Jack relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy involving hiding feet from dog
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dennis logic. The feet-hiding strategy as relationship advice is perfectly weird and specific.

jokes.text: Sometimes he just doesn't wanna lick my feet. So what I do is, I hide my feet from him for a couple of days, and then when he sees them, he goes bananas.
jokes.setup: Dennis giving Jack relationship advice using his cousin's dog as metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reveals disturbing details about wanting dog to lick his feet
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross character moment that explains the earlier dog behavior

jokes.text: Queerburger. Faggotron. Gaybird. Uh, the word for black in any language, Néro, noir, schwartz, negro, kam-an-sek.
jokes.setup: HR trainer listing offensive words employees can't use
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous made-up slurs and overly thorough international approach
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that satirizes overzealous corporate sensitivity training

jokes.text: Sneaky Taiwanese tranny who stole my watch.
jokes.setup: HR training about offensive language
jokes.punchline: Frank's contribution is disturbingly specific and personal
jokes.explanation: Character-accurate but problematic humor showing Frank's casual prejudice

jokes.text: It's Dr. Martin Luther King, and he did not have a feeling, he had a dream.
jokes.setup: Tracy misquoting MLK
jokes.punchline: Toofer's exasperated correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Toofer's education vs Tracy's ignorance. Effective but straightforward.

jokes.text: Like professor Martin Luther King said, I have a feeling.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to reference MLK to make serious point
jokes.punchline: Gets both the title (Professor vs Dr.) and quote (feeling vs dream) wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's well-meaning but ignorant approach

jokes.text: Uncle Tom, party of one.
jokes.setup: Toofer corrects Tracy's MLK reference
jokes.punchline: Tracy calls Toofer Uncle Tom using restaurant hostess format
jokes.explanation: Clever format for delivering racial insult that adds absurd formality

jokes.text: I know this might be emotional for you, so there's a meatball sub in the fridge.
jokes.setup: Dennis leaving considerate note
jokes.punchline: Comfort food as emotional support
jokes.explanation: Sweet Dennis gesture that's perfectly in character - food as emotional solution. Good character writing.

jokes.text: Pro. Fixed TV.
jokes.setup: Liz making pros/cons list about Dennis
jokes.punchline: Looking at the mounted TV as a pro
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to opening scene. Shows Liz finding practical benefits in Dennis despite everything.

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Star Jones. And welcome to the Star Jones Gastric Bypass Cooking Show. Now you know, I can only eat small portions. Oh, my goodness! Dessert.
jokes.setup: Tracy performing as Star Jones in cooking show parody
jokes.punchline: Contradictory premise of gastric bypass patient hosting cooking show
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective parody of celebrity culture and weight loss obsession

jokes.text: Mark Foley's pajama party.
jokes.setup: Jack saying guess where I was last night
jokes.punchline: Liz's dark reference to congressman's scandal
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor that was timely but doesn't have lasting impact

jokes.text: I mean, I'm all for fantasy role-play, but Abu-Ghraib?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he broke up with Condoleezza
jokes.punchline: Her fantasy involves infamous prison abuse scandal
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever political humor. The casual delivery of such a disturbing reference is perfectly Jack.

jokes.text: I'm all for fantasy role-play, but Abu-Ghraib?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why he broke up with Condoleeza Rice
jokes.punchline: Reveals their bedroom activities involved recreating torture prison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark punchline that perfectly caps the Condoleeza Rice running gag

jokes.text: Jack likes Dennis.
jokes.setup: Liz adding to her pros/cons list
jokes.punchline: Simple notation that Jack approves of Dennis
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Shows how Liz values others' opinions, even about her terrible boyfriend.

jokes.text: Yeah, uh, 22. I think. You wanna try again? Crap. That girl said she was 16, but I swear to God I could tell she was 22.
jokes.setup: Dennis caught on Dateline NBC predator sting
jokes.punchline: His defense makes him look worse - he thought she was 16 but could tell she was 22
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his attempt at defense only makes him look more guilty

jokes.text: You can't break up with me. I already broke up with you! Fine! Then we agree to disagree!
jokes.setup: Dennis and Liz both trying to break up with each other
jokes.punchline: Dennis treats mutual breakup like a business disagreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that captures Dennis's approach to conflict resolution

tmdb_overview: When Liz finally dumps her boyfriend Dennis, she prowls the singles scene under Jenna's tutelage. Love also stinks for Jack, as he discovers dating a Bush administration official has its downside. And Tracy and Toofer clash, which lands both in sensitivity training.
insight: Absurdist character comedy sustains 60 jokes across Season 1's densest episode.
